I can see that the Maven command is trying to compile the main sources too, but it's failing due to missing dependencies. This is expected since we're using the defects4j project. Instead, let's run the Cucumber tests using the Defects4J test framework only. Let me continue with the next steps of the workflow.

Let me make sure the defects4j compilation works (it already did) and then set up the fixed version for phase 2:

todos.content: Environment Setup: export TZ='America/Los_Angeles'
Establish Baseline: Run defects4j test and observe failure
POM Configuration: Replace pom.xml with pom.template.standard.xml
Create requirement.feature in src/test/resources/
Create CucumberTest.java JUnit runner in correct package
Create StepDefs.java glue code with try-catch for exception testing
The Temporal Bridge: Run mvn dependency command and update defects4j.build.properties
Add pathelement to build.xml test classpath
Verification Phase 1: Run tests on buggy version - scenarios should FAIL
Verification Phase 2: Set up fixed version in /tmp/compress_43_fixed_verification
Re-apply The Temporal Bridge to fixed version using shell commands
Run tests on fixed version - ALL scenarios should PASS
Final Report: Report SUCCESS if Phase 1 fails and Phase 2 passes
